message:
🐛 Fix usePVCs to aggregate from all clusters when no cluster specified (#472)

* ⚡ Remove unnecessary scroll delay when Pin is off

When Pin is off, we just need to scroll to top immediately.
The previous code set isRestoringRef=true with a 100ms delay
to prevent scroll save, but this is unnecessary since Pin is
off and we won't restore the position anyway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ⚡ Prefetch missing dashboard routes to eliminate load delay

Added DataCompliance, Logs, Arcade, Deploy, AIML, and CICD
to the route prefetch list. These dashboards were loading slowly
on first navigation because their JS chunks weren't being
prefetched during idle time like other dashboards.

Now all dashboard routes are prefetched, ensuring instant
navigation regardless of which dashboard is visited first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Handle 401 errors by logging out and redirecting to login

When API calls receive 401 Unauthorized responses, the token is invalid
or expired. Instead of continuing with a broken session, this change:

- Adds UnauthorizedError class for 401 responses
- Clears token and user cache from localStorage
- Redirects to /login for re-authentication
- Debounces 401 handling to avoid multiple simultaneous logouts from
  parallel API calls

This fixes the issue where cards stay stuck showing skeletons when
the JWT_SECRET changes after backend restart or the token expires.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix --dev flag not affecting frontend URL and JWT secret

The --dev command line flag was being applied AFTER LoadConfigFromEnv()
computed the frontend URL and JWT secret defaults based on DEV_MODE env.
This meant running with --dev still used production defaults.

Fix: Move the default computation to NewServer() where cfg.DevMode has
already been updated by the flag. Now:
- `go run ./cmd/console --dev` correctly uses http://localhost:5174
- `go run ./cmd/console --dev` generates dev JWT secret if not set
- DEV_MODE=true env var still works as before
- FRONTEND_URL and JWT_SECRET env vars still override defaults

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix usePVCs hook not loading due to StrictMode race conditions

React StrictMode double-mounts components, causing fetch requests to be
aborted mid-flight. This was causing the Storage cards to stay stuck in
skeleton loading state.

Fixes:
- Add isMountedRef to track component mount state
- Add cancelled flag in useEffect to prevent state updates after cleanup
- Only reset to loading state if there's no cached data (stale-while-revalidate)

This ensures the hook properly handles the mount/unmount/remount cycle
without losing the fetch result or getting stuck in loading state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix usePVCs to aggregate from all clusters when no cluster specified

When usePVCs() is called without a cluster parameter (e.g., from
StorageOverview card), it now aggregates PVCs from all reachable
clusters via the local agent instead of skipping the agent entirely
and going straight to the backend API.

This fixes storage cards being stuck on skeleton when the backend
is unavailable but the local agent is working.

Changes:
- Aggregate PVCs from all clusters when no cluster specified
- Use parallel fetch for better performance
- Apply same pattern to kubectl proxy fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,8 @@ const UserManagementPage = lazy(() => import('./pages/UserManagement').then(m =>
 const NamespaceManager = lazy(() => import('./components/namespaces/NamespaceManager').then(m => ({ default: m.NamespaceManager })))
 const Arcade = lazy(() => import('./components/arcade/Arcade').then(m => ({ default: m.Arcade })))
 const Deploy = lazy(() => import('./components/deploy/Deploy').then(m => ({ default: m.Deploy })))
+const AIML = lazy(() => import('./components/aiml/AIML').then(m => ({ default: m.AIML })))
+const CICD = lazy(() => import('./components/cicd/CICD').then(m => ({ default: m.CICD })))
 
 // Prefetch all lazy route chunks after initial page load.
 // This runs during idle time so by the time the user navigates,
@@ -75,6 +77,13 @@ if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
       () => import('./components/helm/HelmReleases'),
       () => import('./components/settings/Settings'),
       () => import('./components/gpu/GPUReservations'),
+      // Previously missing - these were loading slowly on first navigation
+      () => import('./components/data-compliance/DataCompliance'),
+      () => import('./components/logs/Logs'),
+      () => import('./components/arcade/Arcade'),
+      () => import('./components/deploy/Deploy'),
+      () => import('./components/aiml/AIML'),
+      () => import('./components/cicd/CICD'),
     ]
     // Stagger imports to avoid blocking the main thread
     chunks.forEach((load, i) => {
@@ -552,6 +561,30 @@ function App() {
             </ProtectedRoute>
           }
         />
+        <Route
+          path="/ai-ml"
+          element={
+            <ProtectedRoute>
+              <OnboardedRoute>
+                <Layout>
+                  <AIML />
+                </Layout>
+              </OnboardedRoute>
+            </ProtectedRoute>
+          }
+        />
+        <Route
+          path="/ci-cd"
+          element={
+            <ProtectedRoute>
+              <OnboardedRoute>
+                <Layout>
+                  <CICD />
+                </Layout>
+              </OnboardedRoute>
+            </ProtectedRoute>
+          }
+        />
         <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />
       </Routes>
       </Suspense>
